errors.description: Attempted to create an AgeGroup with a (tenant_id, organization_id, sport_key, slug) tuple that already exists. If the existing row is soft-deleted, response includes the restore path.
Reached the `age_group_slot` entitlement cap for this tenant (Small=0, Medium=20, Enterprise=unlimited). Response includes tier + upgrade path.
Tenant does not carry the `age_group_custom` entitlement (Small tier). Small tier is locked to the seeded catalog — no customs. Response includes upgrade path.

Attempted to set sport_key on an AgeGroup but the tenant lacks the `age_group_sport_specific` entitlement (Enterprise-only). Response includes upgrade path + the option to leave sport_key null (generic group).

Attempted to archive or delete an AgeGroup that has active inbound references (Team.age_group_id, EventDivision.age_group_id — AthleteEnrollment.age_group_snapshot_id does NOT count because snapshots are self-contained copies). Response includes the reference counts + suggests archiving the referring rows first or transferring them to another AgeGroup.
The AgeGroup is is_default=true within its (tenant, organization, sport_key) tuple. Every tuple must have exactly one default. Promote another AgeGroup to default first, then retry.

The AgeGroup is is_seeded=true (part of the platform's default catalog). Seeded rows may be edited (rename, recolour, bounds edits when entitled) but not deleted — deletion would break the reference baseline the platform ships to every tenant.

Attempted to flip is_default=false without a sibling AgeGroup in the same (tenant, organization, sport_key) tuple carrying is_default=true. System invariant refuses. Warn-severity because the invariant should have been guarded by the observer chain.

Attempted to change min_age_inclusive or max_age_inclusive on an AgeGroup that currently has enrolled Athletes whose new age would fall outside the new bounds (Wave 3+). Response includes the count of affected Athletes + the option to force the change (with an explicit `force=true` param + audit-critical logging).
Same as AGE_GROUP_BOUNDS_INVALIDATE_ATHLETES but for cutoff_date_kind / cutoff_month / cutoff_day changes.
Attempted to mutate sport_key on an AgeGroup with active downstream references. Response includes reference counts + suggests creating a NEW AgeGroup with the new sport_key + migrating consumers.

Attempted to mutate the is_seeded flag. This flag is set once by the platform seeder + never mutated by tenant callers. Security-relevant — surfacing this error means someone tried to promote a custom row to platform-baseline status.
Attempted to create an AgeGroup with is_seeded=true from a non-seeder call site. Same reasoning as AGE_GROUP_IS_SEEDED_IMMUTABLE.

Attempted to change organization_id post-create. Moving an AgeGroup between organizations reshapes its scoping tuple + would silently reassign downstream references. Create a new AgeGroup under the target Organization + migrate consumers instead.
min_age_inclusive > max_age_inclusive OR both outside the platform floor/ceiling [3, 100]. The rule catches it before the observer; this code surfaces on direct service calls.

One of min_age_inclusive < 3 OR max_age_inclusive > 100. The platform floor + ceiling exist to guard against typo entries + to bound the invariant space; tenants who genuinely need broader bounds should file a support ticket.

The combination of cutoff_date_kind + cutoff_month + cutoff_day is inconsistent. `academic_year` requires cutoff_month (day defaults to 1). `custom_date` requires both cutoff_month + cutoff_day. `calendar_year` + `rolling_from_birthday` accept neither (any supplied values are refused as noise).
The slug is one of the platform-reserved values (default, system, seeded, admin, platform). Reserved slugs are used by the seeder + admin tooling; tenants must pick a different slug.
The supplied sport_key is not a valid registered sport identifier. The registry hardens in Wave 3c; before then, sport_key accepts any string matching /^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$/.
Attempted to restore an archived AgeGroup where is_default=true, but the scoping tuple already has an active row with is_default=true. Demote the current default first, then retry the restore.
Attempted to restore an archived AgeGroup where the slug now collides with an active row. Rename the active row or rename the archived row before restoring.

The resolver couldn't find a single matching AgeGroup for the given (DOB, sport_key, on_date). Response includes the candidate list — tenants may have overlapping groups (e.g. an athlete aged 12 might qualify for both U12 and U14 depending on cutoff semantics). Callers must disambiguate explicitly.
